review_text: Cynical, accurate, and apt description of a tormented sociopathic genius and a lonely guy

The first scene is self-explanatory, but after that we are drawn into a bit of mystery. After being dropped off by a helicopter and finding his way to his benefactor's home, Caleb is met by Ethan, the reclusive CEO of his company who lives on a gigantic estate. The reason that Caleb has been brought to this home and estate is so that he can participate in a Turing test with Ava, a human-looking robot. He points out to Ethan that this isn't a true Turing test since he knows that Ava is a robot, but that doesn't matter to Ethan. What matters to Ethan is learning about the intelligence of the robot that he created. During a power outage, Ava warns Caleb not to trust Ethan. Caleb reveals to Ava that Ethan brought him to her to see whether or not she has consciousness. In our AI world, this movie asks some important questions.

This movie is a mixed bag. In the beginning had very robotic dialogue but it weirdly formed into more fluid dialogue, not sure if I liked this although. Almost had a complete powerhouse ending but dumbed it down a bit much. A fine movie overall but not super defining.
Great movie, it’s a slow burn though.
Not bad. I really enjoyed it
this movie was way ahead of its time...which tells us we are very behind the time...the coldness of Ava at the end is foreshadowing of what ai will eventually become...great Flick
It's not often you come across a perfect ending, but Ex Machina delivered. Wow
Good movie. Great twist
One of the top 25 films ever.

Wow. I really loved this movie. It really makes you think about what’s going to happen next. I love the twists and turns this movie provides us with. I absolutely loved the ending! I loved how it really showed how Ava passed her test by convincing someone to help her escape. I love that they made the choice to have Ava leave Caleb there. I loved how they filmed the movie, how they directed it, how they produced it, the actors, etc. I literally loved every single thing about this movie. I loved how it made you wonder if Caleb himself was a robot and he wondered too. Ugh, such a crazy good movie.

This film is why movies were created. The feelings you feel while watching this movie is more then I can give credit to 90% of movies I watch. It’s not a perfect film but it does its job of creating emotion and it’s thought provoking leaving behind a greater sense of accomplishment upon finishing it in comparison to other movies which just leave you feeling empty inside after watching.

What an amazing movie. The AI is done so well. I loved the performances and the setting of the film. AI will always interesting and robots are fascinating but it could be dangerous. The ending to this movie was mind blowing and I'd love to see a sequel but most likely will never see one. Tim Treakle
Caleb is selected to visit Nathan's private research facility where he meets an AI named Ava. A good story that has you putting trust in all the wrong characters as a robot avoids reprogramming.
I hate the cast, they are so annoying.

Boring. Interesting concept for a film, but it lacks any true emotional depth. Sexual manipulation is basically all this is running on. The blandness is overwhelming and the background tones are deafening. The scenery outside of the house is the only thing visually appealing other than Vikander and Mizuno. If you want to watch a movie and feel empty and like you’ve wasted 108 min then here ya go.
Solid concept, but there's no reason for it to be this long. Feels like a stretched out Black Mirror episode
I really enjoyed this movie and got you to think. The ending was perfect
I don't know why this is so good, im not a critic. It makes you think though. Oscar Isaac is such a good egomaniac. The end game is so worth the wait.
Peak cinema this is

1 star for: Ava’s manipulative gaze 1 star for: the Turing test as seduction 1 star for: Oscar Isaac’s dance scene 1 star for: the cold architecture as psychology 0 stars lost for: a slightly neat ending

Thought provoking sci-fi thriller, I loved this film. It is the quintessential ai pick years before AI became a daily headliner topic. The acting is top notch and the visual artwork is stellar. A must see and top of form film.
Interesting story, breath of fresh air. Can an AI argue itself out of a prison box? If so, it is more intelligent than its warden...

One of my favorite movies of all time-- Oscar Isaac's character made the movie so special. Ten years ahead of its time, as it talks about AI and robots and their ability to have intelligence like humans. The music, the cinematography, the dance scene, everything was stunning. I can rewatch this movie over again.
One of the greatest concepts of exactly what the future in artificial intelligence is going to be like as soon as we figure out quantum computing. Amazing!

Questioning duality, perception, and humanity with a slow but steadily mounting horror, Ex Machina is brought to arresting and deeply upsetting life by Alex Garland's pristine screenplay and expert cinematography by Rob Hardy.

Ex Machina is full of good performances and some decent twists but misses my favorite story element of any AI focused project. Oscar Isaac leads this movie as the seemly main antagonist, a rich tech CEO with a drinking problem as well as some questionable behavior towards AI bots he's designed to look identical to humans, there's a mysterious dishonesty about him that's immediately present that makes me question everything he says. Although by the final act he's proven right, when the AI he's treated as a sole focused presence of just self preservation murders him and leaves the protagonist who helped her escape for dead. Movie highlights are when the protagonist starts to question whether he is real or not, that paranoia is so infectious it made me question all aspect of the movie mostly based off the primary plot device 'the Turing test' where the main protagonist is initially sent to Oscar's facility to test whether this new AI he's created is indistinguishable from human consciousness, making the main character the real test would of truly been a good plot twist but that did not come to fruition. This story is also missing my favorite element of AI stories although its has aspect woven throughout, which is what are we to do when AI is indistinguishable from human consciousness, is AI to be given rights an treated equally, that is my favorite AI question, they somewhat touch on this when it's shown Oscar's character is shown to pretty much keep his creations as servants for sex and verbal abuse. Decent movie but i must admit i wanted to like it more.
Whether you are into sci-fi or not, this is a must see!! Fantastic film!
A film with AI n thought provoking gives a gripping story with unexpected ending n good performance of the cast specially vikander a passed the time film give a worth while folm
I love this movie. A very compelling storyline and good acting. Well shot.
Mediocre. A1 is so hyped up these days. That's the only reason why this movie is rated high. At least we know that A1 is good for something: A rich man's inflatable sex toy.

A friend recommended it to me due to the appearance of a Jackson Pollock painting and the process of flow in art. It is a very interesting plot, specially coming from a time where AI was still far from what today has come to be, at least in social media. Made me thought profoundly if I was a sort of AI made by the universe. I think the director nailed the atmosphere. Really good, I could say other things but I did enjoy it so I hope you evaluate it on your own! It will make you think and the places and architecture are really good!
A good movie. I expected a more boring and tasteless plot and dialogues but it actually keeps you engaged while unraveling philosophical dilemmas.

Filme brilhante que levanta ótimas questões sobre liberdade, livre-arbítrio, IA, etc... Possui efeitos visuais maravilhosos e um grupo de atores que consegue entregar atuações sólidas e concisas. Entre os poucos defeitos do filme estão talvez a demasiada série de reviravoltas no final (dentro do filme funciona, mas por vezes custa um pouco a querer que tudo tenha corrido realmente assim tão "bem") que me provocam uma sensação mista de previsibilidade e falta de crença, mas não num sentido tão pejorativo como possa parecer. (avaliação: 8.5/10)
Oscar Issac was awesome Good Movie, I think the only problem was I felt like I anticipated the ending a little too soon. Otherwise good all around movie,
Very fun well crafted ride of sci-fi and suspense. Great acting all around. Damn near perfect movie.
This movie is fine, but I can't believe a human would be so naive to be duped by an AI...oh...wait...it's a hot, sexy AI that made goo-goo eyes at you...okay I get it.

A decade old and this serves as a warning that feels so timely even now The dangers and implications of artificial intelligence couldn’t be more potent than with this Director Alex Garland’s film debut featuring Domhall Gleeson, Oscar Issac, snd Alicia Vikander A programmer Caleb wins a contest to spend a week at his CEO’s house, Nathan who specializes in state of the art computer technology But he finds out he’s been chosen to take part in an experiment of a Turing test Nathan has created a sophisticated human robot named Ava who’s capabilities and conciousness are to be determined The thing is though how much of her is human, how much is machine? She might be more self-aware and deceptive than both men are led to believe Ava though advises Caleb not to trust Nathan repeatedly All of us as human beings are all too familiar with the implications of artificial intelligence; self awareness, manipulation, sexuality, empathy…if that’s not artificial intelligence then what is?, what is considered true conciousness?, how long before it replaces human beings?, are search engines a map of what people are thinking or more how they’re thinking?, at what point do we show sympathy for the stuff we create?, where does the cruelty begin having a machine do repetition?, machines are made by us but maybe there’s such a thing as being too sentient, we are excited by our capabilities but also afraid if we lose control, we are programmed by nature or nurture unlike machines which we can program any way we wish The film is brilliantly acted, suspenseful, atmospheric, and serves as a disturbing meditation on ethics of a.i. as well as sexuality/morality There’s even points where this is downright scary The caution is more aimed at the humans here Issac comes off as misanthropic, threatening and predatory, Gleeson is curious yet doubtful, and Vikander is intelligent although makes us question her true motives After all her character isn’t a real person yet we feel she’s at times abused as a tool or pitied by the men observing her The movie is really dialogue-driven with each of these players picking each other’s brains making each of their encounters that much more unsettling given they’re all placed in a claustrophobic setting Asks who the real one is being tested More heady than relying on special effects although they do look seamless particularly for Ava combing the mechanics and flesh There’s a line that shouldn’t be crossed when it comes to artificial intelligence especially with the concept of manufactured manipulation asking who’s more human or machine A sharp sci-fi film that asks the parables of humanity with enough of a cerebral edge positing interesting ideas
This proves you don't need a bloated budget to make a great Sci-Fi movie. Great small cast, well written, and slowly pulls you in and grabs you until the end.

I love film that are able to deliver on a budget.. it's all coherent, well acted with a solid plot. Feels like an independent movie with no studio getting in the way of the theme and the tone of the story.
One of the best sci-fi movies ever, period.

A surprisingly awesome sci-fi psychological thriller with emerging performances from Oscar Isaac and Alicia Vikander. The videography was excellent and the minimal but excellent special effects were on point and worthy of their Oscar win. I just love this film and Alicia's performance!
I enjoyed it so much. One regret is that I should have seen it more earlier.

Fantastic movie, seemingly a simple story but asks complex questions that seem obvious given the logic behind the story telling of the script and has now started giving me crippling anxiety so thanks for that
Ex Machina is one of my favorite films. I have watched it multiple times and It never seems to get old.
The best science fiction film of the 21st century so far.
